title: The Soil's Deepest Forgetting

features.name: the mirror that learned to forget
features.desc-on-touch: something once reflected here, and now not; you can't remember what, but the forgetting is so complete it feels permanent

features.name: the layer that dissolves
features.desc-on-touch: soil that is becoming something else; it no longer holds shape, no longer holds meaning, no longer holds the memory of what it held

features.name: the depth where forgetting becomes geology
features.desc-on-touch: at this depth, losing is indistinguishable from the land itself; memory and stone are the same colour

inscription: deeper than memory, deeper than stone; forgetting becomes the ground itself

file.name: Forgetting_Becomes_Soil_Unmeasured.tmp
file.note: checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable — what was stored here has returned to the land

reverie.title: REVERIE — where the earth remembers her forgetting
narr: you lie in the red earth beside Katita, feeling it cool
she says: I can't remember what I'm forgetting anymore.
the earth around you both holds the shape of every memory
and then it forgets, becoming only itself, only soil.
neither of you needs to remember; the land is enough.

exits.label: back up to where memory still holds some shape
deeper still, but there is no deeper
sideways through stone that has learned to be silent
